title: Curated stakeholder demo bundles for the five-family wedge
description: Create a curated set of raw-log demo bundles so a stakeholder can paste or load evidence that truthfully maps to the five bounded outage families and immediately understand what NEXUS is proving.
implementation_tasks: Create one curated raw-log bundle for each supported outage family with buyer-legible labels, bounded family mapping, and realistic multiline evidence.
Add additive metadata for each bundle covering what the bundle proves, expected issue family, likely owner, runtime posture, and expected six-agent story.
Keep the bundle corpus static and truthful; do not imply arbitrary log understanding beyond the current five-family wedge.
Add a lightweight plan/reference doc for this phase so future loops can resume the same demo-intake scope cleanly.
done_when: the repo contains a curated demo bundle corpus for all five supported outage families
each bundle declares the expected investigation story and bounded evidence posture
the bundle assets are ready to drive the /inputs UI without inventing new backend contracts

title: Demo bundle contract and browser verification for /inputs
description: Add a failing browser verification path that proves the demo bundle contract is visible and usable before implementing the selector UI.
implementation_tasks: Add browser assertions for a demo bundle selector, bundle proof text, and expected agent-story visibility on /inputs.
Check that selecting a bundle loads logs into the raw intake textarea and updates the parsed evidence surface.
Keep the assertions focused on operator-legible proof, not internal implementation details.
done_when: browser verification fails first because the bundle-selector flow is missing
the tests describe the intended stakeholder-demo behavior clearly

title: Inputs demo bundle selector and proof surfaces
description: Turn /inputs into a strong stakeholder-demo entry point with a bundle selector, bundle proof copy, and explicit explanation of what the chosen logs will demonstrate.
implementation_tasks: Add a visible demo bundle selector to /inputs without removing the free-form paste path.
Render bundle title, what it proves, expected family, likely owner, runtime posture, and next agent path alongside the raw intake preview.
Allow bundle selection to preload the textarea and update the parsed evidence preview immediately.
Keep the UI concise and operator-friendly so the page feels guided rather than overloaded.
done_when: operators can choose a curated demo bundle from /inputs
selecting a bundle updates the raw intake, parsed evidence, and proof narrative
free-form paste remains available and semantically aligned with the curated path

title: Fresh incident demo-origin handoff and output polish
description: Carry the chosen demo-bundle context into the fresh incident workspace so the stakeholder story remains connected after submit, rather than dropping the operator into a generic incident page.
implementation_tasks: Persist the selected bundle context at intake using browser storage keyed to the created nxs_ incident.
Surface a concise demo-origin note in the fresh incident workspace with bundle name, expected family, and what the operator should notice next.
Keep the note additive and truthful; it should guide reading, not fabricate new backend evidence.
Polish the post-submit copy so the transition from /inputs to /incident feels intentional and top-of-page focused.
done_when: fresh incidents created from a curated bundle show their demo origin in the incident workspace
the incident landing copy tells the operator what to inspect next
the input-to-incident transition feels connected rather than abrupt

title: Single stakeholder demo guide for setup, navigation, and expected outcomes
description: Refresh the owner-facing walkthrough so one document explains setup, review order, demo bundles, navigation paths, screen expectations, and the exact story the operator should tell.
implementation_tasks: Update the master operator demo guide with the curated-bundle path, navigation instructions, and expected behavior at each step.
Document the exact demo order, strongest bundle choices, and what each bundle proves about the five-family wedge.
Refresh public doc indexes where the new guide or bundle flow materially changes the story.
done_when: one public doc can be followed end-to-end without outside help
the doc explains how to reach /inputs through the UI and how to run a stakeholder-ready fresh-incident demo
public docs stay aligned with the shipped product wording

title: Validation and control-doc checkpoint for the demo-intake phase
description: Run the full validation set, close the 131–136 phase cleanly, and refresh the repo control docs so the new baseline and next frontier are unambiguous.
implementation_tasks: Run the full validation gate set after items 131-135 land.
Refresh AGENTS, WORKING_STATE, and docs/internal/LOOPS_RUNBOOK to close the old 125-130 wording and record the new demo-intake closure.
Mark backlog-131-plus.json complete if all items close cleanly.
Leave only one narrow next-step note rather than opening a broad new roadmap.
done_when: full validation is green for the new demo-intake baseline
control docs no longer say backlog-125-plus.json is active
the repo truthfully reflects the completed 131-136 phase
